title: artemisia obtusiloba subsp. obtusiloba
intro: Artemisia obtusiloba subsp. obtusiloba, also known as Chinese mugwort or Ai Ye, is a plant species within the genus Artemisia and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of China and other parts of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Artemisia obtusiloba subsp. obtusiloba include its ability to promote digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to make a topical application, and it is also used in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.
